title: What is Asukas Ideal world
wikitext:
[]: What is Asuka's Ideal world?             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Sat Sep 17, 2011 8:54 pm Now, as Eva fans, we are all aware of Asuka's competitive and high prideful personality. Where she has high standards for herself and the people around her, where she expects everyone else to follow in order to somewhat give her a sense of justification to her existence in order to hide a crippling, self-loathing wound. That being the case, I ask: What would be the requirements of Asuka's ideal world, where she herself can be satisfied? What lengths does she need to go, as well as everyone around her, in order to BE satisfied? Does she need to be the Best Eva Pilot in the world and expect everyone to acknowledge her with uttermost praise? Does she need to be an actress so everyone around the world can see how "beautiful" and "talented" she is, and how does she expect them to respond to her? Does she need to be in politics in order to tell the world how she feels it needs to be run? How deep does the "cup" of her life need to be filled in order to be fully satisfied? Does she need to become a "God" herself in order to be happy, being master of space, time, and the people around her? I ask this question because I want to know exactly what people think what kind of life she has trapped herself into. And allow me to give my own opinion on this matter: Is it possible, that due to the utter-crippling nature of her own self-loathing, that the alleged "cup" of her life...has no bottom?

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sat Sep 17, 2011 9:13 pm Asuka wrote: A-and there's a big parade to celebrate how great I am and the mayor gives me a hovercraft! And it has a Super Nintendo inside and a Kool-Aid bar and stupid First can't come!

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sat Sep 17, 2011 9:20 pm I don't think it's possible. She'll always find some flaw, even in a perfect world. Instrumentality probably helped her through that phase, or at least I hope so.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sat Sep 17, 2011 9:31 pm Well, it depends what you mean by ideal. For this response, I'm thinking "What's Asuka's Fantasy Life" Hmm, well I think first of all both her parents are still alive and together. That's a big one. She's probably the Ace at something, if not more than one thing. I'm talking about the childish fantasy of being a race car driver, a doctor, and a lawyer at one time. I don't think being the Ace Eva Pilot is necessarily important, she just needs to be the undisputed Ace, and it needs to garner her inconceivable amounts of attention, meaning she can't be the "Ace" at something relatively minor like Horse Racing. So yea, I'm thinking Ace NASCAR (Or Equivalent) Driver/Player/Pilot/Whatever Parents still alive and together Probably Shinji (and possibly Rei) as her personal handmaidens Doctor or Lawyer or something to compliment being the ace Literally millions of people fawning over the mere sight of her Now, if we're talking about a turning point from the series, where at episode, say, 10, TV Asuka breaks off and her ideal world fills from their.... I'm not quite sure. I think, you might have the right of it in that the "cup" has no bottom. I suppose if everything stayed as it was in Episode 8, with Asuka being the undisputed best Eva pilot, Angel's continuously coming (unlimited number), and Asuka continuously beating them, things might have worked out for her, but her problems would still have existed, and I believe would resurface when her mind was unoccupied for too long (IE When trying to sleep). I hope I answered efficiently. I had a longer post but it was way too off topic so I tried to trim it down. Edit: If you mean, what would it have to be where she wouldn't complain: Impossible. I cannot believe there is any way the human mind, even the healthiest of minds, would not have something to complain about. People in the afterlife probably complain that it's too bloody white and it hurts their eyes.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sat Sep 17, 2011 9:33 pm Like Calvin, she kind of wishes she was dead, but mostly she wishes everyone else was dead. Her ideal world would be the world where everyone was Asuka. But ultimately she gives up on ideal worlds, realizes that she wants to live a real life more than to be happy.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Sep 17, 2011 11:28 pm esselfortium wrote: awesomeness

[]:             Quote Post by merri » Sun Sep 18, 2011 12:40 am TehDonutKing wrote: I don't think it's possible. She'll always find some flaw, even in a perfect world. Instrumentality probably helped her through that phase, or at least I hope so.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Sep 18, 2011 3:58 am Azathoth wrote: Her ideal world would be the world where everyone was Asuka.

[]: The "Best" or not, what does she need? B/             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sun Sep 18, 2011 4:15 am Mr. Tines wrote: Her ideal world would be the world where everyone was Asuka.

[]: Re: The "Best" or not, what does she need? B/             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Sep 18, 2011 4:27 am Alaska Slim wrote: she has no reason to think others care for her.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Sun Sep 18, 2011 4:43 am Mr. Tines wrote: A world in which everyone is Asuka and is on some level aware of that similarity, where as a consequence sympathy is immediate, expected and unforced because the other person is not a sketchily modelled unknown, it becomes a lot easier to love your neighbour as yourself.

[]: Lack of duality equates everything, yet nothing. B/             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sun Sep 18, 2011 5:40 am EvangelionFan wrote: If Asuka is surrounded by copies of herself, her inner need for understanding is fulfilled, and her anxiety of being judged as inadequete in comparison to others is not likely to arise.

[]: Re: Lack of duality equates everything, yet nothing. B/             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Sep 18, 2011 5:51 am Alaska Slim wrote: Is dependent on there being losers

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Sep 18, 2011 5:52 am Azathoth wrote: Her ideal world would be the world where everyone was Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Sun Sep 18, 2011 5:59 am Alaska Slim wrote: And if you're surrounded by clones of yourself, you have a problem, how do you differentiate yourself from the person next to you?

[]: Conscious vs Subconscious/Unconscious Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sun Sep 18, 2011 6:12 am Mr. Tines wrote: Only heroes get to go to Valhalla.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Sep 18, 2011 6:15 am @NemZ I tend to forget the DC accretions when considering the series. In any case, the self-hatred kicks in after she has failed to live up to her own impossible standards. At her nadir, in the bathtub, there is no ideal world left for her.

[]: Hating herself, shame. Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sun Sep 18, 2011 6:25 am Mr. Tines wrote: @NemZ I tend to forget the DC accretions when considering the series.

[]: Re: Conscious vs Subconscious/Unconscious Bl             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Sep 18, 2011 6:35 am Alaska Slim wrote: Are we talking about a consciously recognized ideal world here, or one where her actual needs are met?

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Sun Sep 18, 2011 6:47 am Mr. Tines wrote: I'm taking it as Asuka's daydream world.
summary: []: What is Asuka's Ideal world?             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Sat Sep 17, 2011 8:54 pm Now, as Eva fans, we are all aware of Asuka's competitive and high prideful personality.
